11: Role prompting assigns a specific character, persona, or identity to the language
12: model, often in conjunction with system or contextual prompting. This involves
13: instructing the model to adopt the knowledge, tone, and communication style
14: associated with that role. For example, prompts such as "Act as a travel guide" or "You
15: are an expert data analyst" guide the model to reflect the perspective and expertise
16: of that assigned role. Defining a role provides a framework for the tone, style, and
17: focused expertise, aiming to enhance the quality and relevance of the output. The

25: Effective prompting involves clear distinction of instructions, context, examples, and
26: input for language models. Delimiters, such as triple backticks (\`\`\`), XML tags
27: (\<instruction\>, \<context\>), or markers (---), can be utilized to visually and
28: programmatically separate these sections. This practice, widely used in prompt
29: engineering, minimizes misinterpretation by the model, ensuring clarity regarding the
30: role of each part of the prompt.
